steps.title: Use the diameter form
steps.content: The fastest formula here is circumference equals pi times diameter: $$C=\pi d.$$ The problem gives $C=31\pi$, so $$31\pi=\pi d.$$

steps.title: Cancel $\pi$
steps.content: Divide both sides by $\pi$ to get $$d=31.$$ The diameter is 31 centimeters. You do not need to find the radius first unless you choose to use $C=2\pi r$.
